rationale: Both correctly bisect the failure to PR #4834's intentional NotImplementedError guard, matching yenuo26's thread comment, and both cite the same async_omni.py guard code and propose the same level=1 test fix. However, the actual thread never confirms a fix landed or that the issue is resolved — it ends with Flink-ddd asking to trigger a full CI run to verify, so both candidates' confident 'already fixed, recommend closing' framing overreaches what the thread establishes. Y hedges this appropriately ('if you're still seeing the failure, ensure your CI is running against a tree where...'), which is more calibrated to the genuinely unresolved thread state, while X asserts closure more flatly ('No engine code change is warranted'). X partially offsets this by showing broader awareness of the full test file's regression coverage (relevant to Flink-ddd's ask to run the 'full sleep mode test'), giving it a slight completeness edge, but Y's better-calibrated confidence tips the overall comparison narrowly in its favor.
